description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, sloped array of photovoltaic panels supported by a robust metal framework. The panels are elevated above a patterned platform that appears to serve as both a base and a possible shelter or workspace beneath the array. The structure includes several support columns and a base with geometric black-and-white striping, as well as turquoise and orange accent areas. The roof is composed of multiple solar panels arranged in a grid, with a clear rearward slope for optimal sunlight capture and water runoff. The installation is designed for urban environments, likely providing renewable energy while also offering partial shelter or coverage. The overall design is modern, with a focus on both functionality and visual impact.
description: A large, sloped solar panel array on a patterned platform with metal supports, designed for urban energy generation and partial shelter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped solar panel roof, patterned base, and entry area with visible support columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the solar array, the supporting columns, and the open space beneath the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar array, the patterned platform, and the orange accent area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the structure's depth, support columns, and the open area beneath the panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 10-15 degrees, facing rearward, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with steel posts and metal framework
attribute_array_configuration: single row of approximately 6-8 large panels arranged side by side
